title: Opportunity Cost Awareness

content.question: What are you saying yes to that's costing you a better 'no'?
content.equation: True Cost = Direct + Best Alternative Lost
sop: List your current top 3 time or resource commitments.
For each, name the single best alternative you're giving up by keeping it (see calculation, page 5)
Estimate the Direct cost and the value of the Best Alternative Lost in dollars or hours (see calculation, page 5)
Sum them to get the True Cost, compare it honestly to what the commitment is actually returning.
Drop or renegotiate the one commitment where True Cost clearly exceeds its return.
questions: What are you saying yes to that's costing you a better 'no'?
What's the best opportunity you'd have to turn down if the thing you're currently doing showed up tomorrow instead?
Which recurring commitment on your calendar have you never actually compared against its next-best alternative?

definitions.def: The value of the next best alternative you give up when you make a choice.

content.equation_example: Direct Cost equals $500 (what you paid), Best Alternative Lost equals $1,200 (value of what you gave up to do this instead). True Cost equals $1,700. Higher means the decision cost far more than the price tag showed. Lower means the price tag was close to the real cost.
content.closing_statement: The price tag is never the whole cost.

caption:
Principle Nº 153: Opportunity Cost Awareness

Every "yes" is a hidden "no".

Ask yourself: What are you saying yes to that's costing you a better 'no'?
